entities.name: RESOURCE MANAGEMENT DOMAINS
entities.description: Biophysical units serving as a common denominator for the synthesis of information to assess and monitor land quality

entities.name: LAND QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of land that is assessed and monitored using biophysical units

entities.name: FOOD PRODUCTION SYSTEMS
entities.description: Homogeneous systems of food production identified through information technology

entities.name: FARM SYSTEMS
entities.description: Systems including socioeconomic and cultural contexts from the national to the farm level

entities.name: CROP SYSTEMS
entities.description: Systems of crops whose performance is evaluated in varied environments

entities.name: HIERARCHY THEORY
entities.description: The theoretical framework used to develop the proposed hierarchy for information integration

entities.name: BIODIVERSITY
entities.description: A biological attribute that can be optimized based on planning area objectives

entities.name: ECOSYSTEM HEALTH
entities.description: The overall condition of an ecosystem that can be optimized based on planning area objectives

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The condition of the soil that is maintained based on planning area objectives

entities.name: COMMODITY PRODUCTION
entities.description: The production of agricultural commodities to be optimized within a planning area

entities.name: LAND RESOURCE ASSESSMENTS
entities.description: The process of evaluating land resources, often using RMDs

entities.name: FOREST ECOSYSTEMS
entities.description: Ecosystems that require a refined approach for land resource assessments over long time periods

entities.name: TEMPORAL VARIATIONS
entities.description: Changes over time that are de-emphasized in the RMD approach assuming equilibrium

entities.name: INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The technological advancement driving the interest in defining food production systems
